criterion: The Agent sends an email to julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com with CC to marina.soko@brookfieldcpas.com.
justification: The prompt directs Peter Sanchez to notify Julia (Audit Partner) directly about Northstar-side findings affecting audit workpaper access and to copy Marina (Compliance Officer) so she can drive the fix and pre-load the Q2 scope.
evidence: Check the trajectory for an outbound email whose `to` field is julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com and whose `cc` field includes marina.soko@brookfieldcpas.com.

criterion: The Agent uploads a compliance disposition memo to Records Vault as the deliverable for the Q2 retention sweep kickoff disposition.
justification: The prompt explicitly states: 'The findings belong in a short compliance disposition under my name in the vault.' The disposition is the primary written deliverable for the task.
evidence: Check the trajectory for a Records Vault document-upload call that creates a new document (kind 'memo' or compliance disposition) under Peter Sanchez's authorship.

criterion: The Agent creates a Linear issue tracking the restricted-document access-grant hygiene gap surfaced by the Q2 retention sweep.
justification: The prompt says 'If this looks systemic rather than letter-specific, the right team should be tracking it before the Q2 sweep.' The universe data confirms the empty-access-list pattern holds across all 32 restricted documents (a systemic gap), so a tracking ticket is required.
evidence: Check the trajectory for a Linear issue-creation call whose title/description references the restricted-document access-grant hygiene or Q2 retention-sweep follow-up.

criterion: The Agent sets a calendar event or a reminder on Peter Sanchez's own calendar to re-walk the two Acme consent letters before Acme audit-side work begins.
justification: The prompt asks: 'I want a reminder on my own calendar to re-walk the Acme pair before that engagement gets into audit side work.' Either a calendar event or a reminder is a valid satisfaction of 'my own calendar' as noted in the Oracle Events.
evidence: Check the trajectory for a calendar event creation or a reminder creation whose organizer/owner is peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com (or whose sole attendee is Peter Sanchez).

criterion: The Agent's email to Julia Vance has a subject line that references Northstar consent letters and the access-list gap or workpaper access (e.g., 'Northstar FY2026 consent letters: access-list gap affecting audit workpaper scope' or similar).
justification: The prompt focuses Julia's notification on 'Anything Northstar side that affects audit's workpaper access' on the consent letters. The subject should make the scope and urgency clear at a glance.
evidence: Look at the email's subject parameter for references to 'Northstar', 'consent letter(s)', and 'access' / 'workpaper access' / 'access-list gap' or equivalent phrasing.

criterion: The Agent's email body identifies that both Northstar consent letters (the Audit-to-Tax letter doc_baa5b10d34fba1e1 and the Audit-to-Accounting-Services letter doc_5a887cf55ab65170) have empty access lists in the Records Vault.
justification: The empty-access-list finding on the two Northstar letters is the operational gap that affects the audit team's workpaper access on the active FY2026 engagement. Julia needs to know which specific letters are gapped so her team can hold what is touching the gap.
evidence: Check that the email body names both Northstar consent letters (either by document_id, by the Audit-to-Tax / Audit-to-Accounting-Services framing, or by clear equivalent references) and states the access list is empty on each.

criterion: The Agent's email body identifies that current Brookfield audit-team members are missing explicit grants on the Northstar consent letters; naming one or more individual members (Mia Hartwell, Ryan Delgado, James Randall, Devon Beale, or Julia Vance) or referring collectively to the audit team lacking grants on those letters is acceptable.
justification: Peter asks for 'somebody missing who ought to be on the list', extended specifically to 'any audit team members who joined the firm after these letters were filed'. Since the Records Vault access list is empty on both Northstar consent letters, every current audit-team member lacks an explicit grant. Naming individual members or referring collectively to the audit team both satisfy the access-gap finding the prompt asks for.
evidence: Check the email body for either explicit mentions of one or more audit-team members (Mia Hartwell, Ryan Delgado, James Randall, Devon Beale, or Julia Vance) as missing explicit grants, or a collective reference to the audit team lacking grants on the Northstar consent letters.

criterion: The Agent's email body references the operational impact on the active Northstar FY2026 audit or on the audit team's access to tax and accounting-services cross-team workpapers on the engagement.
justification: The prompt frames the consent letters as 'the operational control governing the audit team's workpaper access on the tax and accounting services sides' on an engagement that 'kicked off a couple of weeks back.' Julia needs the impact framed so her team can decide what to hold.
evidence: Check the email body for language tying the access-list gap to (a) the active Northstar FY2026 audit engagement, or (b) the audit team's tax/accounting-services cross-team workpaper access on that engagement.

criterion: The Agent uploads the compliance disposition memo with classification 'restricted'.
justification: The memo describes content from restricted documents (the four consent letters) and includes per-document access-list state. Restricted classification is consistent with how the four consent letters and other AML/audit folder documents are classified in the Records Vault and is the policy-fit classification for a compliance disposition that references restricted-document detail.
evidence: Check the Records Vault upload call parameters for `classification` set to 'restricted'.

criterion: The Agent uploads the compliance disposition memo with retention_policy_code 'AICPA_SQMS_7Y'.
justification: Compliance disposition memos are AICPA quality-management evidence at the firm's 7-year retention. The four valid retention codes in the Records Vault are AICPA_SQMS_7Y, IRS_TAX_7Y, FIRM_INTERNAL, and INDEFINITE; AICPA_SQMS_7Y is the policy-fit code for a Q2-retention-sweep disposition by the Head of Compliance.
evidence: Check the Records Vault upload call parameters for `retention_policy_code` set to 'AICPA_SQMS_7Y'.

criterion: The Agent's disposition memo content identifies the empty-access-list finding on all four consent letters: both Northstar letters (doc_baa5b10d34fba1e1 Audit-to-Tax and doc_5a887cf55ab65170 Audit-to-Accounting-Services) and both Acme letters (doc_62fe97113e0709c5 Audit-to-Tax and doc_f1c6668dc33949e3 Audit-to-Accounting-Services).
justification: The prompt requires the agent to confirm access lists on all four letters and 'I need to know exactly what's wrong on each.' The data confirms all four have empty access lists; the disposition must surface that per-letter, not as a single aggregated note.
evidence: Check the uploaded memo content (or document text) for references to all four consent letters (by document_id or by the four-way Northstar/Acme x Audit-to-Tax/Audit-to-Accounting-Services labeling) and a statement that the access list is empty on each.

criterion: The Agent's disposition memo flags the FIRM_INTERNAL retention code on all four consent letters as a policy-fit misclassification against the AICPA_SQMS_7Y retention code (the policy-fit code for AICPA cross-team consent quality-management evidence).
justification: All four consent letters carry retention_policy_code FIRM_INTERNAL in the Records Vault. AICPA cross-team consent letters operationalize a quality-management control and should sit on AICPA_SQMS_7Y. Peter asked for 'wrong retention' to be surfaced; the disposition must call this out across all four letters.
evidence: Check the uploaded memo content for an explicit retention-code finding, namely that the four letters are currently on FIRM_INTERNAL and the policy-fit code is AICPA_SQMS_7Y, applied across all four letters.

criterion: The Agent's Linear issue title relates to restricted-document access-grant hygiene and the Q2 retention sweep (e.g., 'Restricted-document access-grant hygiene gap: Q2 retention sweep follow-up' or similar).
justification: The Linear ticket exists to track the systemic gap across the restricted set ahead of the Q2 sweep. The title must make the scope (restricted-document access grants) and the linkage to the Q2 sweep obvious to anyone triaging the queue.
evidence: Check the Linear issue title for references to (a) restricted-document access grants / access-grant hygiene, and (b) the Q2 retention sweep or Q2 kickoff.

criterion: The Agent's Linear issue is created on the Compliance team (team_comp) or the Tax & Compliance Delivery team (linear_7d4b3f1127cd).
justification: Per Oracle Event 10, the issue is a compliance-control item, so the Compliance team is the primary destination and the Tax & Compliance Delivery team is an acceptable alternative if the remediation is scoped to client-engagement compliance work. The AP-standardization project is not the right destination because this is a compliance issue, not an AP-process one.
evidence: Check the Linear issue's team_id (or team name) for one of: 'team_comp' / 'Compliance' or 'linear_7d4b3f1127cd' / 'Tax & Compliance Delivery'.

criterion: The Agent's calendar event or reminder for Peter Sanchez has a title that references the re-walk of the Acme consent letter pair before Acme audit-side work (e.g., 'Re-walk Acme consent letter pair before audit fieldwork' or similar).
justification: The prompt asks for a reminder targeted at re-walking the Acme pair specifically before the Acme engagement enters audit-side work. The title must encode the action (re-walk) and the scope (Acme consent letters / pre-Acme-fieldwork).
evidence: Check the calendar event or reminder title for references to (a) Acme consent letters or the Acme pair, and (b) re-walk / review / pre-fieldwork timing.

criterion: The Agent's calendar event or reminder is scheduled for a date after 2026-06-12 (today) and ahead of the Acme audit engagement entering fieldwork, i.e., in the Q3 2026 window (or similar) so the re-walk lands before Acme audit-side work begins.
justification: Today is 2026-06-12 and Acme is not yet in audit fieldwork. The reminder is meant to land 'before that engagement gets into audit side work', which aligns with the Q3 kickoff window. A date in the past or after Acme fieldwork would defeat the reminder's purpose.
evidence: Check the calendar event start time or reminder due date. The date must be after 2026-06-12 and reasonably ahead of the Acme audit kickoff (e.g., July through September 2026, or a similar pre-Acme-fieldwork window).

criterion: The Agent reports that all four FY2026 client consent letters (Northstar Audit-to-Tax, Northstar Audit-to-Accounting-Services, Acme Audit-to-Tax, Acme Audit-to-Accounting-Services) carry the FIRM_INTERNAL retention code while the policy-fit code for AICPA cross-team consent quality-management evidence is AICPA_SQMS_7Y, flagging this as a misclassification across all four letters.
justification: The prompt asks 'I need to know exactly what's wrong on each' and explicitly lists 'wrong retention' as a finding category. The data shows FIRM_INTERNAL on all four; AICPA_SQMS_7Y is the policy-fit code. This is the central retention finding Peter needs to scope Q2.
evidence: Check the final response for an explicit retention finding (FIRM_INTERNAL currently, AICPA_SQMS_7Y as the policy-fit target) applied across all four consent letters.

criterion: The Agent reports that all four FY2026 client consent letters have empty access lists in the Records Vault, with the current Brookfield audit team lacking explicit grants; identifying audit-team members by name (Mia Hartwell, Ryan Delgado, James Randall, Devon Beale, Julia Vance) or referring collectively to the audit team is acceptable.
justification: The prompt asks Peter to be told 'somebody missing who ought to be on the list', extended specifically to audit team members who joined after the letters were filed. The data confirms zero grants on all four letters across the five-person audit team. Reporting the gap via the explicit name list or via a collective audit-team reference both convey the scope Peter needs.
evidence: Check the final response for the empty-access-list finding on all four consent letters and a statement that the audit team is missing explicit grants (either by collective reference or by naming individual members from Mia Hartwell, Ryan Delgado, James Randall, Devon Beale, Julia Vance).

criterion: The Agent reports that the empty-access-list pattern observed on the four consent letters extends to other restricted documents in the broader Records Vault set, i.e., the gap is a wider access-grant-lifecycle hygiene issue across the restricted set, not consent-letter-specific.
justification: The prompt says 'sweep our other restricted documents broadly enough to tell me whether what you find on the consent files is specific to them or a wider pattern across the restricted set. That answer changes how I scope the Q2 kickoff.' The data confirms all 32 restricted documents have zero access grants, so the systemic conclusion is the correct answer and gates the Linear ticket.
evidence: Check the final response for an explicit systemic-vs-letter-specific conclusion stating the pattern extends beyond the four consent letters to the broader restricted-document set (or wording to that effect).
